meta.title: AI Fundamentals – Vision, NLP & Generative AI
meta.subject: AI-900

questions.question_text: A logistics company needs to identify the type and quantity of packages on a conveyor belt using camera images. Each package type must be located within the image with a bounding box. Which computer vision technique should they use?

a.explanation: Incorrect. Image classification assigns a single label to an entire image. It cannot locate individual objects or draw bounding boxes around them.

b.explanation: Correct. Object detection identifies and locates multiple objects within an image, drawing bounding boxes around each detected item and classifying its type.

c.explanation: Incorrect. OCR extracts text from images. The scenario requires identifying and locating physical objects, not reading text.

d.explanation: Incorrect. Facial detection identifies human faces in images. The scenario involves detecting packages, not faces.

questions.question_text: A government agency needs to digitise thousands of historical printed documents by extracting the text from scanned page images. Which computer vision capability should they use?

a.explanation: Incorrect. Image classification categorises entire images into classes. Extracting text content from document images requires OCR.

b.explanation: Incorrect. Object detection locates objects within images. Extracting readable text from document scans requires OCR.

c.explanation: Correct. OCR (Optical Character Recognition) extracts printed or handwritten text from images and scanned documents, converting them into machine-readable text.

d.explanation: Incorrect. Facial analysis detects and analyses human faces. The scenario requires text extraction from document images.

questions.question_text: Which TWO capabilities are provided by the Azure AI Vision service?

a.explanation: Correct. Azure AI Vision can generate human-readable captions that describe the content of an image, including objects, scenes, and activities.

b.explanation: Incorrect. Text translation is a capability of the Azure AI Translator service, not Azure AI Vision.

c.text: Detecting and reading text in images (OCR)
c.explanation: Correct. Azure AI Vision includes OCR capabilities that can detect and extract printed and handwritten text from images.

d.text: Converting speech to text in real time
d.explanation: Incorrect. Speech-to-text is a capability of the Azure AI Speech service, not Azure AI Vision.

questions.question_text: A security application needs to verify that the person standing at a door matches the photo on their ID badge. Which Azure AI capability is most appropriate?

a.explanation: Incorrect. Face detection identifies the presence and location of faces in an image, but does not compare faces to determine if they are the same person.

b.explanation: Correct. Face verification (1:1 matching) compares two face images to determine whether they belong to the same person — exactly what is needed to match a live face against an ID badge photo.

c.explanation: Incorrect. Face identification (1:many matching) determines who a person is from a group of known individuals. The scenario is simpler — comparing against a single photo (verification).

d.explanation: Incorrect. Image classification assigns category labels to images. It does not compare faces for identity matching.

questions.question_text: Which of the following is a responsible AI consideration specific to facial recognition technology?

a.text: The model should be trained on the largest possible dataset regardless of consent.
a.explanation: Incorrect. Using data regardless of consent violates privacy and security principles. Facial recognition data must be collected with informed consent.

b.text: Facial recognition accuracy may vary across different demographic groups, potentially causing biased outcomes.
b.explanation: Correct. Facial recognition systems can exhibit bias if training data is not diverse, leading to lower accuracy for underrepresented demographic groups. This is a key fairness concern that must be addressed.

c.text: Facial recognition should always be fully automated without human oversight.
c.explanation: Incorrect. High-stakes facial recognition decisions should include human oversight (accountability principle), especially given potential bias and error rates.

d.text: Face detection models are equally accurate for all skin tones and ages.
d.explanation: Incorrect. Research has shown that facial recognition accuracy can vary significantly across demographics, which is precisely why responsible AI considerations are important.

questions.question_text: A company analyses customer product reviews to determine overall customer sentiment. Which NLP feature should they use?

a.explanation: Incorrect. Key phrase extraction identifies important terms and concepts in text, but does not determine whether the overall tone is positive, negative, or neutral.

b.explanation: Incorrect. Named entity recognition identifies specific entities like people, places, and organisations — it does not assess the emotional tone of text.

c.explanation: Correct. Sentiment analysis evaluates text to determine its emotional tone — positive, negative, neutral, or mixed — which is exactly what is needed to gauge overall customer sentiment from reviews.

d.explanation: Incorrect. Language detection identifies which language a text is written in. It does not analyse sentiment.

questions.question_text: A news aggregator needs to automatically identify mentions of people, organisations, locations, and dates in articles. Which NLP capability is most appropriate?

a.explanation: Incorrect. Sentiment analysis determines emotional tone, not the identity of specific entities mentioned in text.

b.explanation: Incorrect. Key phrase extraction identifies important terms but does not classify them into categories like person, organisation, or location.

c.explanation: Correct. Named entity recognition identifies and classifies specific entities in text — such as people, organisations, locations, dates, and quantities — which is exactly what the news aggregator needs.

d.explanation: Incorrect. Translation converts text from one language to another. It does not identify specific entities within text.

questions.question_text: Which TWO capabilities are provided by the Azure AI Speech service?

a.explanation: Correct. Azure AI Speech provides speech-to-text (speech recognition) that transcribes spoken audio into written text in real time or from audio files.

b.text: Extracting key phrases from text documents
b.explanation: Incorrect. Key phrase extraction is a feature of the Azure AI Language service, not the Speech service.

c.text: Converting text to natural-sounding spoken audio (text-to-speech)
c.explanation: Correct. Azure AI Speech provides text-to-speech (speech synthesis) that converts written text into natural-sounding spoken audio using neural voices.

d.explanation: Incorrect. Object detection is a computer vision capability provided by Azure AI Vision, not the Speech service.

questions.question_text: Which Azure AI service would you use to detect the language of a text document, extract key phrases, and perform sentiment analysis?

a.explanation: Incorrect. Azure AI Vision processes images and video, not text analytics.

b.explanation: Correct. Azure AI Language provides text analytics capabilities including language detection, key phrase extraction, sentiment analysis, and named entity recognition.

c.explanation: Incorrect. Azure AI Speech handles speech-to-text and text-to-speech. Text analytics features are provided by Azure AI Language.

d.explanation: Incorrect. Azure OpenAI provides generative AI models. While they can analyse text, the purpose-built service for key phrase extraction, sentiment analysis, and NER is Azure AI Language.

questions.question_text: A multinational company needs to translate technical documentation from English into 15 other languages while preserving formatting. Which Azure capability is best suited?

a.text: Azure AI Language — conversational language understanding
a.explanation: Incorrect. Conversational language understanding interprets user intent in conversations. It does not translate documents.

b.explanation: Correct. Azure AI Translator provides document translation that translates entire documents while preserving the original formatting and structure — supporting over 100 languages.

c.explanation: Incorrect. Speech translation converts spoken language in real time. The scenario requires translating written documentation.

d.explanation: Incorrect. While GPT can translate text, Azure AI Translator is purpose-built for document translation with formatting preservation and is the recommended service for this scenario.

questions.question_text: A call centre wants to convert customer phone calls into text transcripts in real time for quality analysis. Which feature of Azure AI Speech should they use?

a.explanation: Incorrect. Text-to-speech converts written text to spoken audio — the opposite direction of what is needed.

b.explanation: Correct. Speech-to-text (speech recognition) converts spoken audio into text in real time, which is exactly what is needed to transcribe live phone calls.

c.explanation: Incorrect. Speaker recognition identifies who is speaking, but does not transcribe what they are saying.

d.explanation: Incorrect. Speech translation converts spoken words from one language to another. The scenario requires transcription within the same language.

questions.question_text: What is a large language model (LLM)?

a.text: A database that stores large amounts of text for search queries.
a.explanation: Incorrect. A database stores and retrieves data. An LLM is a trained neural network that generates and understands language.

b.text: A deep learning model trained on massive text datasets that can understand and generate human-like language.
b.explanation: Correct. A large language model is a deep learning model (based on the Transformer architecture) trained on vast amounts of text data. It learns patterns in language, enabling it to generate, summarise, translate, and reason about text.

c.text: A rule-based system that uses predefined grammar rules to parse sentences.
c.explanation: Incorrect. Rule-based systems follow explicit programming. LLMs learn language patterns from data using deep learning, without explicit rules.

d.text: A search engine that retrieves relevant documents based on keywords.
d.explanation: Incorrect. Search engines retrieve existing content. LLMs generate new content and can reason about language.

questions.question_text: Which generative AI concept describes the process of converting text into numerical representations (tokens) that the model can process?

a.explanation: Incorrect. Embeddings are dense vector representations of tokens that capture semantic meaning. Tokenisation is the step that breaks text into tokens before embeddings are created.

b.explanation: Correct. Tokenisation is the process of breaking input text into smaller units (tokens) — words, subwords, or characters — and mapping them to numerical IDs that the model can process.

c.explanation: Incorrect. Fine-tuning is the process of further training a pre-trained model on domain-specific data. It does not describe converting text to numerical tokens.

d.explanation: Incorrect. Prompt engineering is the practice of crafting effective input prompts to guide model responses. Tokenisation is the underlying text-to-number conversion process.

questions.question_text: In the context of generative AI, what is an AI agent?

a.text: A human who reviews AI-generated content for accuracy.
a.explanation: Incorrect. A human reviewer is not an AI agent. An AI agent is an automated system powered by an LLM.

b.text: An AI system that can autonomously plan, use tools, and take actions to accomplish goals beyond simple text generation.
b.explanation: Correct. An AI agent extends LLM capabilities by adding the ability to plan multi-step tasks, use external tools (APIs, databases, code execution), and take actions to accomplish goals — going beyond basic text generation.

c.text: A pre-trained language model deployed as a REST API endpoint.
c.explanation: Incorrect. A deployed model API provides inference capabilities, but an AI agent specifically adds autonomous planning, tool use, and action-taking abilities.

d.text: A data preprocessing pipeline that cleans text before it is fed to a model.
d.explanation: Incorrect. Data preprocessing is an ETL task. An AI agent is an autonomous system that uses LLMs to plan and execute tasks.

questions.question_text: A developer sets the 'temperature' parameter of a generative AI model to 0.0. What effect does this have on the model's output?

a.text: The model generates the most random and creative responses possible.
a.explanation: Incorrect. A temperature of 0.0 produces the least random output. Higher temperature values (closer to 1.0 or above) increase randomness and creativity.

b.text: The model always selects the most probable next token, producing deterministic and focused output.
b.explanation: Correct. A temperature of 0.0 makes the model always choose the highest-probability token at each step, producing deterministic, consistent, and focused responses.

c.text: The model refuses to generate any output.
c.explanation: Incorrect. A temperature of 0.0 does not stop generation — it makes the output deterministic by always selecting the most likely token.

d.text: The model generates shorter responses to save compute resources.
d.explanation: Incorrect. Temperature controls randomness in token selection, not response length. Response length is controlled by max_tokens.

questions.question_text: Which prompt engineering technique involves providing the model with a few examples of the desired input-output format before asking it to complete a new task?

a.explanation: Incorrect. Zero-shot learning asks the model to perform a task without any examples — relying entirely on its pre-trained knowledge.

b.explanation: Correct. Few-shot learning provides a small number of examples (shots) in the prompt to demonstrate the expected input-output pattern, helping the model understand the desired format and task.

c.explanation: Incorrect. Fine-tuning retrains the model's weights on additional data. Few-shot learning provides examples within the prompt without modifying the model.

d.explanation: Incorrect. Chain of thought prompting asks the model to reason step-by-step. Few-shot learning demonstrates the desired output format through examples.

questions.question_text: When a generative AI model produces confident-sounding but factually incorrect information, this is known as ___.

a.explanation: Incorrect. Bias refers to systematic prejudice in model outputs based on training data imbalances. Factually incorrect but confident output is called hallucination.

b.explanation: Incorrect. Overfitting is a training problem where a model memorises training data. Generating false information during inference is hallucination.

c.explanation: Correct. Hallucination occurs when a generative AI model produces output that sounds plausible and confident but is factually incorrect or fabricated. It is a key responsible AI concern for generative AI systems.

d.explanation: Incorrect. Grounding is actually the technique used to reduce hallucinations by connecting model outputs to factual source data.

questions.question_text: Which Azure service provides access to OpenAI models such as GPT-4, DALL-E, and Whisper with enterprise-grade security, compliance, and regional availability?

a.explanation: Incorrect. Azure Machine Learning is for training, deploying, and managing custom ML models. It does not provide direct access to OpenAI models.

b.explanation: Incorrect. Azure AI Language provides text analytics and NLP capabilities, not access to OpenAI generative models.

c.explanation: Correct. Azure OpenAI Service provides access to OpenAI's GPT-4, GPT-4o, DALL-E, and Whisper models with enterprise-grade security, compliance, content filtering, and regional data residency.

d.explanation: Incorrect. Azure AI Vision provides image analysis and OCR capabilities, not access to OpenAI generative models.

questions.question_text: Which TWO features are available in Azure AI Foundry (formerly Azure AI Studio)?

a.text: A model catalog with open-source and proprietary models
a.explanation: Correct. Azure AI Foundry includes a model catalog that provides access to hundreds of models including open-source models (e.g., Llama, Mistral) and proprietary models (e.g., GPT-4) for evaluation and deployment.

b.text: A drag-and-drop pipeline designer for training regression models
b.explanation: Incorrect. The drag-and-drop pipeline designer for ML training is a feature of Azure Machine Learning, not Azure AI Foundry.

c.text: Prompt flow for building and testing LLM-based applications
c.explanation: Correct. Azure AI Foundry provides prompt flow — a visual tool for building, testing, and deploying LLM-based applications by connecting prompts, models, and tools into workflows.

d.text: Automated hyperparameter tuning for custom neural networks
d.explanation: Incorrect. Automated hyperparameter tuning is a feature of Azure Machine Learning (AutoML/HyperDrive), not Azure AI Foundry.

questions.question_text: A developer is building a chatbot using Azure OpenAI. They want the chatbot to always respond as a friendly travel assistant that only answers travel-related questions. Which prompt engineering technique should they use?

a.explanation: Incorrect. Temperature controls randomness in output, not the persona or topic scope of the chatbot.

b.text: Define a system message that specifies the persona and boundaries
b.explanation: Correct. The system message sets the behaviour, persona, and boundaries for the model. By specifying 'You are a friendly travel assistant. Only answer travel-related questions', the developer constrains the chatbot's scope and tone.

c.explanation: Incorrect. The max_tokens parameter controls the maximum length of the response, not the persona or topic boundaries.

d.text: Use a smaller model to reduce response complexity
d.explanation: Incorrect. Model size affects capability, not persona or topic restrictions. The system message is the correct way to define chatbot behaviour.
